chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P.No.D-612 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A. No.2883/2025. 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For hearing of M.A. No.2415/2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 12.11.2025 Mr. Wishan Das, Advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Om Parkash H. Karmani, Advocate for the respondent No.4. Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh along-with SIP / I.O Muhammad Ashraf PS Pithoro. == 1. Urgency application is granted. 2to4. Petitioners are present in Court along with their learned counsel. Petitioner No.1 submits that she has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2 on account of her own freewill and choice, without any compulsion, coercion or inducement. She further submits that consequent upon the said marriage, her father (respondent No.4) became annoyed and has lodged a false FIR against her husband (petitioner No.2). Learned counsel for respondent No.4 submits that under the Hindu Law, a person is not permitted to contract more than one marriage during the subsistence of the first. However, in the present case, it is submitted that petitioner No.2, namely Sarwan Kumar, has contracted his third marriage, which, according to the learned counsel, is in violation of the principles of Hindu Law. Mr. Muhammad Ashraf, Investigating Officer, Police Station Pithoro, is present and submits that he has recorded the statement of petitioner No.1, wherein she has categorically denied the allegations contained in the FIR and has not supported it. The I.O. further submits that in view of her statement, he will dispose of the case under ‘C’ Class and would submit a report before the learned concerned Magistrate within fifteen days.

Muhammad Ashraf, Investigating Officer, Police Station Pithoro, is present and submits that he has recorded the statement of petitioner No.1, wherein she has categorically denied the allegations contained in the FIR and has not supported it. The I.O. further submits that in view of her statement, he will dispose of the case under ‘C’ Class and would submit a report before the learned concerned Magistrate within fifteen days. Upon submission of such report, the learned Magistrate shall pass an appropriate order in terms of the statement of lady petitioner. Petitioners and their counsel are satisfied with such statement made by the investigating officer and seek disposal of the petition in the above terms. Accordingly, this petition is disposed of in the terms of statement of lady petitioner. Police official present is directed to provide protection to the petitioners in accordance with law. However, the respondent No.4 is at liberty to approach the concerned Family Court for redressal of his grievance on the issue he raised if so advised to him. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish
